worker_spec.summary: Create the checked-in Phase 0 inventory artifact for the orchestrator-constructor refactor.
worker_spec.target_state_description: Run the inventory sweeps described in plan/structural-refactors/01_orchestrator_constructor/03_migration_plan.md Phase 0 and write a one-file artifact containing every current call site of `Orchestrator::new`, `Orchestrator::with_llm`, `Orchestrator::with_checkpoint_manager`, and `.llm_backend()`. The artifact must list exact file:line references so later workers do not need to rediscover them. Do not edit Rust code in this feature.

steps.fail_reason: constructor inventory artifact missing
constructor inventory artifact does not record all required query categories

worker_spec.summary: Introduce the test-only OrchestratorBuilder behind the `test-support` feature without breaking existing callers.
worker_spec.target_state_description: Add `OrchestratorBuilder` exactly as a test-only construction surface. `builder.rs` must exist. The builder type, its impl blocks, and its export from `lib.rs` must all be guarded by `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-support"))]`. Add `test-support = []` to `crates/swell-orchestrator/Cargo.toml`. This phase is add-only: do not delete old constructors yet.

steps.fail_reason: workspace did not compile after builder introduction
builder.rs missing
test-support cfg gates missing on builder surface
test-support feature missing from Cargo.toml

worker_spec.summary: Migrate the orchestrator crate’s own tests from old constructors to OrchestratorBuilder.
worker_spec.target_state_description: Using the inventory artifact, replace orchestrator-crate test call sites of `Orchestrator::new()`, `Orchestrator::with_llm(...)`, and `Orchestrator::with_checkpoint_manager(...)` with `OrchestratorBuilder` usage. Restrict edits to the orchestrator crate’s test surfaces and any minimal supporting code required there. Do not touch daemon or integration tests yet.

steps.fail_reason: workspace did not compile after orchestrator test migration
orchestrator crate tests regressed during builder migration

worker_spec.summary: Finish Phase 2 by migrating remaining non-orchestrator tests and integration call sites to OrchestratorBuilder.
worker_spec.target_state_description: Use the inventory artifact to migrate every remaining test/integration call site off `Orchestrator::new`, `Orchestrator::with_llm`, and `Orchestrator::with_checkpoint_manager`. Update dev-dependencies only where needed for `test-support`. At the end of this feature there must be zero test/integration call sites using the old constructor surface.

steps.fail_reason: workspace did not compile after cross-crate builder migration
old orchestrator constructor calls remain in test or integration code

steps.cmd: cargo test -p swell-validation -- --test-threads=4 && cargo test -p swell-integration-tests --test full_cycle_wiring
steps.fail_reason: cross-crate tests regressed during builder migration

worker_spec.summary: Delete the legacy constructor surface and make `Orchestrator::new(llm)` the sole production constructor.
worker_spec.target_state_description: Change `llm_backend` from `Option<Arc<dyn LlmBackend>>` to `Arc<dyn LlmBackend>`. Delete `Orchestrator::new()` and `Orchestrator::with_checkpoint_manager(...)`. Rename/replace `with_llm` so `pub fn new(llm)` is the only public constructor. Update `llm_backend()` to return `Arc<dyn LlmBackend>` directly. Update `crates/swell-daemon/src/server.rs` to call `Orchestrator::new(llm)`. Ensure the builder delegates to the new constructor. Do not add any placeholder Option fields for future subsystems. Update any `.is_some()` or `.unwrap()` call sites on `llm_backend()` to match the new non-optional return type.

steps.fail_reason: workspace did not compile after constructor flip
llm_backend is still optional after constructor flip
expected exactly one public Orchestrator::new constructor
public with_* constructor still exists on Orchestrator
workspace tests regressed after constructor flip

worker_spec.summary: Prove the builder is unreachable from production via compile-fail and production-build checks.
worker_spec.target_state_description: Add the `compile_fail` doctest on `Orchestrator` proving parameterless construction does not compile. Add the CI/build check described in the plan so production build artifacts do not expose `OrchestratorBuilder`. Pick one deterministic validation path suitable for this repo’s platform/tooling and encode it clearly. Do not weaken the gating requirement.

steps.fail_reason: doctests did not prove compile-fail constructor invariant
production daemon build failed during builder-gating checks
OrchestratorBuilder leaked into production binary

worker_spec.summary: Finish Phase A by removing compile-time-redundant wiring tests, codifying constructor policy, and proving all anti-patterns are blocked mechanically.
worker_spec.target_state_description: Apply the redundancy audit exactly: remove only the compile-time-redundant constructor-presence wiring/witness tests, keep the runtime-behavior keepers, and update the named AGENTS/docs surfaces. Also ensure the tree contains no anti-pattern backdoors from `02_target_architecture.md`: no wrapped Option holes, no required-subsystem Defaults, no public `with_*` setters on `Orchestrator`, no disabled/null constructor substitutes, and no `OrchestratorConfig` with `Option` holes. This is the final Phase A hard gate.

steps.fail_reason: workspace did not compile during Phase A final gate
wrapped Option anti-pattern still present
required subsystem still has Default backdoor
public with_* setter still exists on Orchestrator
disabled or null constructor backdoor still present on refactor-01 surface
OrchestratorConfig Option-hole anti-pattern present

steps.cmd: cargo test -p swell-integration-tests --test full_cycle_wiring && cargo test --workspace --doc
steps.fail_reason: wiring suite or doctests regressed during Phase A final gate

worker_spec.summary: Introduce the WiringReport/WiringState contract and the orchestrator wiring-manifest surface.
worker_spec.target_state_description: Add the shared `WiringReport` trait and `WiringState` enum, then add the `Orchestrator` wiring-manifest surface that returns report entries for every required/load-bearing subsystem named in the plan. It is acceptable for not-yet-landed subsystems to report `DISABLED(<reason>)`; do not implement those future subsystems here.

steps.fail_reason: workspace did not compile after WiringReport introduction

steps.cmd: rg -n 'trait WiringReport|enum WiringState|fn wiring_manifest' crates/swell-core crates/swell-orchestrator --type rust

required subsystem names missing from wiring manifest surface

worker_spec.summary: Print the wiring manifest once at daemon startup and enforce strict mode on degraded/disabled state.
worker_spec.target_state_description: Hook the manifest into the daemon startup path so it prints exactly once with explicit state tokens and a `[wiring-check]` summary line. Implement `SWELL_STRICT=1` refusal behavior when any subsystem reports degraded or disabled. Update daemon guidance so future subsystem additions must update the manifest output.

steps.fail_reason: workspace did not compile after startup-manifest wiring

worker_spec.summary: Add the manifest-specific integration proof to the wiring suite.
worker_spec.target_state_description: Add the wiring-manifest integration assertion described by the plan: the full-cycle wiring suite must prove the manifest includes every required subsystem by name. Keep the test mechanical and bounded to named subsystem entries.

Internal helpers may still use `anyhow` if needed, but the public boundary must become typed. Keep the feature limited to the daemon crate public boundary and its tests.
